lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Mr. Ishiguro, have you ever found one of your books at a second-hand bookstore?
lines.translation: 石黑先生，您有没有在二手书店发现过您的一本书？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Yes.
lines.translation: 是的。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: That kind of thing is difficult.
lines.translation: 那种情况很难处理。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: If they've got my book there, I think "Well, this is an insult!
lines.translation: 如果那儿有我的书，我会想：“嗯，这是一种侮辱！

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Somebody didn't want to keep my book."
lines.translation: 有人不想保留我的书。”

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: But if it's not there, I feel it's an insult too.
lines.translation: 但如果那里没有，我也觉得这是一种侮辱。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I think, "Why aren't people exchanging my book?
lines.translation: 我会想：“为什么人们不交流我的书？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Why isn't it in this store?"
lines.translation: 为什么这家店里没有？”

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Does being a writer require a thick skin?
lines.translation: 当作家需要脸皮厚吗？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Yes.
lines.translation: 是的。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: For example, my wife can be very harsh.
lines.translation: 例如，我妻子可能非常严厉。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I began working on my latest book, The Buried Giant, in 2004, but I stopped after I showed my wife a little section.
lines.translation: 我2004年开始创作我的最新小说《被掩埋的巨人》，但在给我妻子看了一小部分后就停笔了。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: She thought it was rubbish.
lines.translation: 她觉得那是垃圾。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Even after you won a Booker Prize?
lines.translation: 即使在您获得了布克奖之后？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: She is not intimidated at all, and she criticises me in exactly the same way she did when I was first unpublished and I was starting.
lines.translation: 她一点也不觉得敬畏，她批评我的方式和我最初未发表作品、刚起步时完全一样。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: But you would never compromise on your vision?
lines.translation: 但您绝不会在您的愿景上妥协？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: No. I wouldn't ever compromise on the essential, the ideas, or the themes.
lines.translation: 不。我绝不会在核心本质、思想或主题上妥协。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: This isn't really what my wife is trying to criticise me about.
lines.translation: 这实际上并不是我妻子想要批评我的地方。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: It's always about execution.
lines.translation: 总是关于执行方面。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: So why did you put your book The Buried Giant aside for so long?
lines.translation: 那么您为什么把《被掩埋的巨人》这本书搁置了这么久？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Apparently, you started working on it over ten years ago.
lines.translation: 显然，您在十多年前就开始写了。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I've often stopped writing the book and left it for a few years.
lines.translation: 我经常停笔写这本书，把它搁置几年。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: And by the time I come back to it, it may have changed.
lines.translation: 等我再回来写它时，它可能已经变了。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Usually my imagination has moved on, and I can think of different contexts or a different way to do it.
lines.translation: 通常我的想象力已经向前发展了，我能想到不同的背景或不同的处理方式。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: What does it feel like when you finally finish a book?
lines.translation: 当您最终完成一本书时是什么感觉？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: It's funny you ask that, because I never have this moment when I feel "Ah, I've finished!"
lines.translation: 您问这个挺有趣的，因为我从来没有这种觉得“啊，我写完了！”的时刻。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I watch footballers at the end of the match, you know; the whistle goes and they've won or lost.
lines.translation: 您知道，我在比赛结束时看着足球运动员；哨声一响，他们要么赢了，要么输了。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Until then, they've been giving everything they have, and at that moment they know it's over.
lines.translation: 在此之前，他们一直在付出自己的一切，而就在那一刻，他们知道比赛结束了。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: It's funny for an author.
lines.translation: 对作家来说这挺有趣的。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: There's never a finishing whistle.
lines.translation: 永远没有结束的哨声。

lines.speaker: Q1.
lines.text: How would the man feel if he found his book in a second-hand bookstore?
lines.translation: 如果男人在二手书店发现自己的书，他会有什么感觉？

lines.speaker: Q2.
lines.text: What does the man's wife think of his books?
lines.translation: 男人的妻子是怎么看他的书的？

lines.speaker: Q3.
lines.text: What does the man do when he engages in writing?
lines.translation: 男人在从事写作时会做什么？

lines.speaker: Q4.
lines.text: What does the man want to say by mentioning the football match?
lines.translation: 男人提到足球比赛想说明什么？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: According to a study of Race & Equity in Education, black athletes are dropping out of college across the country at alarming rates.
lines.translation: 根据一项关于教育中种族与公平的研究，全国范围内黑人运动员正以惊人的比例从大学辍学。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: With us to talk about the findings in the study is Washington Post columnist Kevin Blackstone.
lines.translation: 和我们一起讨论这项研究发现的是《华盛顿邮报》专栏作家凯文·布莱克斯通。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Good morning!
lines.translation: 早上好！

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Good morning.
lines.translation: 早上好。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: How are you?
lines.translation: 你好吗？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Fine, thank you.
lines.translation: 很好，谢谢。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: What is new that you found in this study?
lines.translation: 你在这项研究中有什么新发现？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Well, this is Shaun Harper's study.
lines.translation: 嗯，这是肖恩·哈珀的研究。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: And he points out that on major college campuses across the country, black males make up less than 3 percent of undergraduate enrollments.
lines.translation: 他指出，在全国各地的主要大学校园里，黑人男性在本科生入学人数中占比不到3%。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Yet, when you look at their numbers or percentages on the revenue-generating sports teams of football and basketball, they make up well into 50-60 percent of those teams.
lines.translation: 然而，当你看看他们在创收的橄榄球和篮球运动队中的人数或比例时，他们在这类球队中占到了50%到60%以上。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: So the idea is that they are really there to be part of the revenue-generating working class of athletes on campus, and not necessarily there to be part of the educating class as most students in other groups are.
lines.translation: 因此，这种观点是，他们真正在那里是作为校园内创收的运动员劳工阶层的一部分，而不一定是像其他群体中的大多数学生那样作为受教育阶层的一部分。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Compared with other groups, I think the numbers in this group at those 65 schools are something like just barely more than half of the black male athletes graduate at all.
lines.translation: 与其他群体相比，我认为在那65所学校中，这个群体的数字大概是勉强超过一半的黑人男运动员能毕业。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Exactly.
lines.translation: 完全正确。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: And what's really bad about this is these athletes are supposedly promised at least one thing as reward for all their blood and sweat.
lines.translation: 而这其中真正糟糕的是，这些运动员本应获得至少一样东西，作为对他们所有血汗的奖励。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: And that is a college degree, which can be a transformative tool in our society when you talk about upward mobility.
lines.translation: 那就是大学学位，当你谈论向上流动时，在我们的社会中它可以是一个变革性的工具。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: And that's really the troubling part about this.
lines.translation: 这才是这件事真正令人不安的地方。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Well, this has been talked about so much, really, in recent years.
lines.translation: 嗯，近些年关于这个的话题真的谈论得太多了。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Why hasn't changed?
lines.translation: 为什么没有改变？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Well, I think one of the reasons it hasn't changed is because there is really no economic pressure to change this.
lines.translation: 嗯，我认为没有改变的原因之一是因为确实没有改变此事的经济压力。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: All of the incentive is really on winning and not losing on the field or on the court.
lines.translation: 所有的激励实际上都在于在赛场或球场上赢而不是输。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Coaches do not necessarily have the incentive to graduate players.
lines.translation: 教练并不一定有让球员毕业的动力。

lines.speaker: Q5.
lines.text: What are the speakers talking about?
lines.translation: 说话者在谈论什么？

lines.speaker: Q6.
lines.text: What is the new finding about black male athletes in the study?
lines.translation: 研究中关于黑人男运动员的新发现是什么？

lines.speaker: Q7.
lines.text: What is the graduation rate of black male athletes?
lines.translation: 黑人男运动员的毕业率是多少？

lines.speaker: Q8.
lines.text: What accounts for black athletes' failure to obtain a college degree according to the man?
lines.translation: 根据男人的说法，是什么导致黑人运动员未能获得大学学位？

lines.text: America's holiday shopping season starts on Black Friday, the day after Thanksgiving.
lines.translation: 美国的假日购物季始于黑色星期五，也就是感恩节的第二天。

lines.text: It is the busiest shopping day of the year.
lines.translation: 这是一年中最繁忙的购物日。

lines.text: Retailers make the most money this time of year, about 20 to 30 percent of annual revenue.
lines.translation: 零售商在一年中的这个时候赚的钱最多，约占全年收入的20%到30%。

lines.text: About 136 million people will shop during the Thanksgiving holiday weekend.
lines.translation: 大约有1.36亿人将在感恩节假期周末购物。

lines.text: More and more will shop online.
lines.translation: 越来越多的人会在网上购物。

lines.text: In an era of instant information, shoppers can use their mobile phones to find deals.
lines.translation: 在即时信息的时代，购物者可以使用手机寻找优惠。

lines.text: About 183.
8 million people will shop on Cyber Monday, the first Monday after Thanksgiving.
lines.translation: 约1.838亿人将在网络星期一，即感恩节后的第一个星期一购物。

lines.text: More than half of all holiday purchases will be made online.
lines.translation: 超过一半的假日购买将在网上进行。

lines.text: One in five Americans will use a tablet or smartphone.
lines.translation: 五分之一的美国人将使用平板电脑或智能手机。

lines.text: Online spending on Black Friday will rise 15 percent to hit 2.
lines.translation: 黑色星期五的在线支出将增长15%，达到27亿美元。

lines.text: 7 billion dollars this year.
lines.translation: 今年达到27亿美元。

lines.text: Cyber Monday spending will increase 12 percent to 3 billion dollars.
lines.translation: 网络星期一的支出将增加12%，达到30亿美元。

lines.text: For many, shopping online was "a more comfortable alternative" than crowded malls.
lines.translation: 对许多人来说，相比拥挤的商场，网上购物是“一种更舒适的选择”。

lines.text: The shift to online shopping has had a big impact on traditional shopping malls.
lines.translation: 向网上购物的转变对传统购物中心产生了巨大影响。

lines.text: Since 2010, more than 24 shopping malls have closed and an additional 60 are struggling.
lines.translation: 自2010年以来，已有超过24家购物中心关闭，另有60家正在苦苦挣扎。

lines.text: However, Fortune says the weakest of the malls have closed.
lines.translation: 然而，《财富》杂志称，最弱的购物中心已经关闭。

lines.text: The sector is thriving again.
lines.translation: 该行业再次繁荣起来。

lines.text: The International Council of Shopping Centers said 94.
lines.translation: 国际购物中心理事会表示，到2014年底，94.2%的购物中心已满租

lines.text: 2 percent of malls were full, or occupied with shops by the end of 2014.
lines.translation: 或被商店占据。

lines.text: That is the highest level in 27 years.
lines.translation: 这是27年来的最高水平。

lines.text: Economist, Gus Faucher, said lower unemployment and rising wages could give Americans more money to spend.
lines.translation: 经济学家格斯·福彻表示，失业率下降和工资上涨可能会给美国人带来更多的钱用于消费。

lines.text: The average American consumer will spend about 805 dollars on gifts.
lines.translation: 美国普通消费者将花费约805美元购买礼物。

lines.text: That's about 630.
lines.translation: 在11月到12月之间，总额约为6305亿美元，

lines.text: 5 billion dollars between November and December, an increase of 3.
lines.translation: 比去年增长了3.7%。

lines.text: 7 percent from last year.
lines.translation: 比去年增长了3.7%。

lines.speaker: Q9.
lines.text: What is the speaker mainly talking about?
lines.translation: 说话者主要在谈论什么？

lines.speaker: Q10.
lines.text: How many people will shop on Cyber Monday?
lines.translation: 有多少人会在网络星期一购物？

lines.speaker: Q11.
lines.text: What does Fortune say about traditional shopping malls?
lines.translation: 《财富》杂志怎么评价传统购物中心？

lines.speaker: Q12.
lines.text: What is said to account for the increase number of shoppers?
lines.translation: 据说是什么导致了购物者数量的增加？

lines.text: For years, many of us have relied on antibiotic use to treat various infections.
lines.translation: 多年来，我们中的许多人一直依赖使用抗生素来治疗各种感染。

lines.text: And the reality is that antibiotics have been responsible for saving millions of lives since penicillin, one of the earliest antibiotics who's first used on a clinical basis 70 years ago.
lines.translation: 而现实是，自青霉素——最早在70年前首次用于临床的抗生素之一——问世以来，抗生素挽救了数百万人的生命。

lines.text: However, today is a new era in which taking antibiotics can cause some very dangerous and potentially life-threatening situations.
lines.translation: 然而，今天是一个新时代，服用抗生素可能会导致一些非常危险且具有潜在生命威胁的情况。

lines.text: In fact, you may have heard about the new "superbugs", which are antibiotic-resistant bacteria that have developed as a result of overprescribed antibiotics.
lines.translation: 事实上，你可能听说过新的“超级细菌”，它们是由于过度开具抗生素处方而产生的耐抗生素细菌。

lines.text: In the past, health experts warned us that the day would come in which it would become very difficult to provide medical care for even common problems, such as lung infection or severe sore throat.
lines.translation: 过去，健康专家警告我们，总有一天，即使是常见的疾病（如肺部感染或严重的喉咙痛），也很难提供医疗护理。

lines.text: And apparently that day has come, because seemingly routine operations such as knee replacements are now much more hazardous due to the looming threat of these infections.
lines.translation: 显然，那一天已经到来，因为膝关节置换等看似常规的手术，由于这些感染的潜在威胁，现在变得危险得多。

lines.text: The problem has grown into such epidemic proportions that this severe strain of resistant bacteria is being blamed for nearly 700,000 deaths each year throughout the world.
lines.translation: 这个问题已经发展到了类似流行病的程度，以至于这种严重的耐药细菌菌株被指责为全世界每年近70万例死亡的原因。

lines.text: And unfortunately, health experts worry that the number will rise to 10 million or more on a yearly basis by 2050.
lines.translation: 不幸的是，健康专家担心，到2050年，这个数字将上升到每年1000万或更多。

lines.text: With such a large life-threatening epidemic, it is sad to say that only 1.
lines.translation: 面对如此大规模危及生命的流行病，可悲的是，美国国立卫生研究院仅将1.2%

lines.text: 2 percent of budgetary money for the National Institutes of Health is currently being spent on research to tackle this problem.
lines.translation: 的预算资金用于应对这一问题的研究。

lines.text: This is a far cry from the funds necessary for a problem of such magnitude.
lines.translation: 这与解决如此大规模问题所需的资金相去甚远。

lines.speaker: Q13.
lines.text: What do we learn about the "superbugs"?
lines.translation: 关于“超级细菌”我们了解到了什么？

lines.speaker: Q14.
lines.text: What is the result of the overuse of antibiotics?
lines.translation: 过度使用抗生素的结果是什么？

lines.speaker: Q15.
lines.text: What is most urgently needed for tackling the large life-threatening epidemic, according to the speaker?
lines.translation: 根据说话者的说法，应对这种大规模危及生命的流行病最迫切需要什么？

lines.text: This is the reason you are here in a university.
lines.translation: 这就是你们在大学里的原因。

lines.text: You are here to be educated.
lines.translation: 你们在这里是为了接受教育。

lines.text: You are here to understand thinking better and to think better yourself.
lines.translation: 你们在这里是为了更好地理解思考，以及自己更好地思考。

lines.text: It's not a chance you are going to have throughout your lifetime.
lines.translation: 这不是你一生中经常会有的机会。

lines.text: For the next few years, you have a chance to focus on thinking.
lines.translation: 在接下来的几年里，你有一个专注于思考的机会。

lines.text: I think about some of the students who took advantage of their opportunities in a university.
lines.translation: 我想起了一些在大学里充分利用机会的学生。

lines.text: One of the stories I always like to tell is about a freshman seminar that I had a chance to teach at Harvard when I was President of the university.
lines.translation: 我总是喜欢讲的一个故事是关于我在担任哈佛大学校长时有机会教授的一门新生研讨课。

lines.text: I taught a seminar on globalization and I assigned a reading that I had written about global capital flows.
lines.translation: 我教授一门关于全球化的研讨课，并布置了一篇我写的关于全球资本流动的阅读材料。

lines.text: And as I did each week, I asked one of the students to introduce the reading.
lines.translation: 像我每周做的那样，我让一名学生介绍这篇阅读材料。

lines.text: And this younger man in October of his freshman year said something like the following.
lines.translation: 这个在新生一年级十月份的年轻小伙子说了大概如下的话。

lines.text: "The reading by President Simons on the flow of capital across countries, it was kind of interesting, but the data did not come close to supporting the conclusions."
lines.translation: “西蒙斯校长关于资本跨国流动的这篇阅读材料，有点意思，但数据远不能支持其结论。”

lines.text: And I thought to myself, "What a fantastic thing this was!"
lines.translation: 我心里想：“这是多么美妙的一件事啊！”

lines.text: How could somebody who had been there for five weeks tell the person who had the title President that he didn't really know what he was talking about?
lines.translation: 一个在那里才待了五个星期的人，怎么能告诉一个拥有校长头衔的人，说他其实不知道自己在说什么呢？

lines.text: And it was a special moment.
lines.translation: 那是一个特殊的时刻。

lines.text: Now, I don't want to be misunderstood.
lines.translation: 现在，我不想被误解。

lines.text: I explained to my student that I actually thought he was rather more confused than I was, and I argued back.
lines.translation: 我向我的学生解释说，实际上我认为他比我更困惑，然后我反驳了他。

lines.text: But what was really important about that was the universities stand out as places that really are about the authority of ideas.
lines.translation: 但这其中真正重要的是，大学脱颖而出，成为真正关乎思想权威的地方。

lines.text: You see it in faculty members who are pleased when their students make a discovery that undermines a cherished theory that they had put forward.
lines.translation: 你会在教职员工身上看到这一点，当他们的学生有发现，破坏了他们自己提出并珍视的理论时，他们会感到高兴。

lines.text: I think of another students I had who came to me one morning, one evening actually, walked into my office and said that I had written a pretty good paper, but that it had five important mistakes and that he wanted a job.
lines.translation: 我想起了我的另一个学生，他有一天早上——其实是一天晚上——来找我，走进我的办公室说我写了一篇相当不错的论文，但它有五个重要错误，他想要一份工作。

lines.text: You could debate whether they actually were mistakes, but you couldn't debate that young man's hunger to learn.
lines.translation: 你可以争论那些是否真的是错误，但你无法争论那个年轻人对学习的渴望。

lines.text: You could not debate that that young man was someone who wanted to make a difference in economics and he is today a professor of economics and his works are more cited as an economist than any other economist in the world.
lines.translation: 你无法争论那个年轻人是一个想在经济学领域有所建树的人，而他今天是一名经济学教授，作为一名经济学家，他的著作被引用的次数超过了世界上任何其他经济学家。

lines.speaker: Q16.
lines.text: What does the speaker say about a university?
lines.translation: 说话者关于大学说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q17.
lines.text: What do we learn from the speaker's stories about universities?
lines.translation: 从说话者关于大学的故事中我们了解到了什么？

lines.speaker: Q18.
lines.text: What does the speaker see in the young man who challenged his paper?
lines.translation: 说话者在那个挑战他论文的年轻人身上看到了什么？

lines.text: Psychological research shows we consistently underestimate our mental powers.
lines.translation: 心理学研究表明，我们一直低估了自己的心智能力。

lines.text: If you think this does not apply to you, then here is a simple test to show you're wrong.
lines.translation: 如果你认为这不适用于你，那么这里有一个简单的测试来证明你错了。

lines.text: Write down the names of all the American states you can remember.
lines.translation: 写下你能记住的所有美国州名。

lines.text: Put the list away, and then set yourself the same task a week later.
lines.translation: 把单子收起来，一周后给自己设定同样的任务。

lines.text: Provided you have not cheated by consulting an atlas, you will notice something rather surprising.
lines.translation: 只要你没有通过查阅地图册作弊，你会注意到一些相当令人惊讶的事情。

lines.text: The two lists will contain roughly the same number of states, but they will not be identical.
lines.translation: 这两份清单将包含大致相同数量的州，但它们不会完全相同。

lines.text: Some names will have slipped away, but others will have replaced them.
lines.translation: 有些名字会被遗忘，但其他名字会取代它们。

lines.text: This suggests that somewhere in your mind you may well have a record of virtually every state.
lines.translation: 这表明在你脑海深处，你可能保留了几乎每个州的记录。

lines.text: So it is not really your memory letting you down, just your ability to retrieve information from it.
lines.translation: 所以真正让你失望的不是你的记忆力，而只是你从中提取信息的能力。

lines.text: We would remember a lot more if we had more confidence in your memories and knew how to use them properly.
lines.translation: 如果我们对自己的记忆更有信心，并知道如何正确使用它们，我们会记住更多。

lines.text: One useful tip is that things are more likely to be remembered if you are in exactly the same state and place as you were when you learn them.
lines.translation: 一个有用的提示是，如果你处于学习它们时完全相同的状态和地点，你更有可能记住这些事情。

lines.text: So if you are a student who always reviews over black coffee, perhaps it would be sensible to prime yourself with a cup before the exam.
lines.translation: 所以，如果你是一个总是一边喝黑咖啡一边复习的学生，也许在考试前喝一杯让自己进入状态是明智的。

lines.text: If possible, you should also try to learn information in the room where it is going to be tested.
lines.translation: 如果可能的话，你也应该尽量在将要进行考试的房间里学习信息。

lines.text: When you learn is also important.
lines.translation: 你学习的时间也很重要。

lines.text: Lots of people swear they can absorb new information more efficiently at some times of the day than at others.
lines.translation: 许多人确信他们在一天中的某些时候吸收新信息的效率比其他时候更高。

lines.text: Research shows this is not just imagination.
lines.translation: 研究表明这不仅仅是想象。

lines.text: There is a biological rhythm for learning, though it affects different people in different ways.
lines.translation: 学习存在一种生物节律，尽管它对不同的人影响方式不同。

lines.text: For most of us, the best plan is to take in new information in the morning and then try to consolidate it into memory during the afternoon.
lines.translation: 对我们大多数人来说，最好的计划是在早晨吸收新信息，然后在下午尝试将其巩固到记忆中。

lines.text: But this does not apply to everyone, so it is essential to establish your own rhythm.
lines.translation: 但这并不适用于每个人，所以建立你自己的节奏是必不可少的。

lines.speaker: Q19.
lines.text: What does psychological research show according to the speaker?
lines.translation: 根据说话者的说法，心理学研究显示了什么？

lines.speaker: Q20.
lines.text: What do we find about the two lists of names of American states?
lines.translation: 关于两份美国州名的清单我们发现了什么？

lines.speaker: Q21.
lines.text: What is the useful tip the speaker gives?
lines.translation: 说话者给出的有用提示是什么？

lines.speaker: Q22.
lines.text: What does the speaker advise us to do to improve our learning efficiency?
lines.translation: 说话者建议我们做什么来提高学习效率？

lines.text: Hello, today I'm going to talk about poverty.
lines.translation: 大家好，今天我要谈谈贫困问题。

lines.text: Poverty has become a critical issue in today's world.
lines.translation: 贫困已成为当今世界的一个关键问题。

lines.text: It concerns not only us sociologists, but also economists, politicians and business people.
lines.translation: 它不仅关系到我们社会学家，也关系到经济学家、政治家和商界人士。

lines.text: Poverty has been understood in many different ways.
lines.translation: 人们对贫困有很多不同的理解。

lines.text: One useful way is to distinguish between three degrees of poverty: extreme poverty, moderate poverty, and relative poverty.
lines.translation: 一种有用的方法是将贫困分为三种程度：极端贫困、中度贫困和相对贫困。

lines.text: The first type of poverty is extreme poverty; it's also called absolute poverty.
lines.translation: 第一种贫困是极端贫困；它也被称为绝对贫困。

lines.text: In extreme poverty, households cannot meet basic needs for survival.
lines.translation: 在极端贫困中，家庭无法满足生存的基本需求。

lines.text: People are chronically hungry.
lines.translation: 人们长期处于饥饿状态。

lines.text: They are unable to access safe drinking water, let alone health care.
lines.translation: 他们无法获得安全的饮用水，更不用说医疗保健了。

lines.text: They cannot afford education for their children.
lines.translation: 他们负担不起孩子的教育费用。

lines.text: In short, people who live in extreme poverty do not have even the minimum resources to support themselves and their families.
lines.translation: 简而言之，生活在极端贫困中的人们甚至没有最低限度的资源来养活自己和家人。

lines.text: Where does extreme poverty occur?
lines.translation: 极端贫困发生在什么地方？

lines.text: Well, you can find it only in developing countries.
lines.translation: 嗯，你只能在发展中国家找到它。

lines.text: Well, what about moderate poverty?
lines.translation: 那么，中度贫困呢？

lines.text: Unlike extreme poverty, moderate poverty generally refers to conditions of life in which basic needs are met but barely.
lines.translation: 与极端贫困不同，中度贫困通常指基本需求勉强得到满足的生活条件。

lines.text: People living in moderate poverty have the resources to keep themselves alive, but only at a very basic level.
lines.translation: 生活在中度贫困中的人们拥有维持自己生存的资源，但仅仅处于非常基本的水平。

lines.text: For example, they may have access to drinking water, but not clean, safe drinking water.
lines.translation: 例如，他们可能有饮用水，但不是干净、安全的饮用水。

lines.text: They may have a home to shelter themselves, but it does not have power supply, a telephone or plumbing.
lines.translation: 他们可能有一个可以遮风挡雨的家，但里面没有电力供应、电话或自来水管道。

lines.text: The third kind of poverty is relative poverty.
lines.translation: 第三种贫困是相对贫困。

lines.text: Relative poverty is generally considered to be a household income level, which is below a given proportion of average family income.
lines.translation: 相对贫困通常被认为是一个低于平均家庭收入给定比例的家庭收入水平。

lines.text: The relatively poor live in high-income countries, but they do not have a high income themselves.
lines.translation: 相对贫困的人生活在高收入国家，但他们自己的收入并不高。

lines.text: The method of calculating the poverty line is different from country to country, but we can say that basically a family living in relative poverty has less than a percentage of the average family income.
lines.translation: 计算贫困线的方法因国家而异，但我们可以说，基本上，生活在相对贫困中的家庭的收入低于平均家庭收入的一定百分比。

lines.text: For example, in the United States, a family can be considered poor if their income is less than 50% of the national average family income.
lines.translation: 例如，在美国，如果一个家庭的收入低于全国平均家庭收入的50%，就可以被认为是贫困的。

lines.text: They can meet their basic needs but they lack access to cultural goods, entertainment, and they cannot afford higher education.
lines.translation: 他们可以满足基本需求，但他们缺乏获得文化产品和娱乐的机会，而且他们负担不起高等教育。

lines.speaker: Q23.
lines.text: What is the speaker's profession?
lines.translation: 说话者的职业是什么？

lines.speaker: Q24.
lines.text: Where does extreme poverty occur according to the speaker?
lines.translation: 根据说话者的说法，极端贫困发生在什么地方？

lines.speaker: Q25.
lines.text: What does the speaker say about families living in relative poverty in the United States?
lines.translation: 说话者关于生活在美国的相对贫困家庭说了什么？
